series.title: Series
series.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this series?

thread.title: Threads
thread.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this thread?

import.pasteDescription: Paste thread data copied from the web

translate.removeAllConfirm: Remove all translations for this thread?

llm.title: LLM Settings
llm.batchBlocksDesc: Send all blocks in one API call per response (saves API calls)

sections.title: Layout
items: The screen has 3 columns: Series | Threads | Viewer.
Series are optional folders that group threads.
Threads contain responses (posts).
Drag column borders to resize widths.

sections.title: Adding Data
items: {icon:paste} Paste: Paste 2ch/5ch text copied from the web to parse it.
{icon:upload} Import: Load a .laa file.
{icon:plus} Create: Create an empty series/thread.
Use the {icon:plus} button in the viewer header to add responses manually. Specify a sequence number to insert at a specific position.

sections.title: Editing & Deleting
items: {icon:pencil} Edit series/thread/response (switch locale in dropdown).
{icon:trash} Delete items. Deleting a series also deletes all its threads.
Drag & drop threads onto series items to move them.
Drop onto 'All' to unassign from a series.

sections.title: Translation
items: First configure LLM provider, API key, and model in Settings.
Translate button in viewer header: translates thread title + all responses in parallel.
Per-response translate button: translates a single response.
Hold Shift + click to force re-translate (ignores existing translations).
Drag-select text to reveal a selection translate button.
Remove Translation button deletes translation for a specific response.
Adjust concurrent translations in Settings (default: 3).

sections.title: Export
items: {icon:download} Export series or thread as a .laa file.
.laa files can be shared with other users.
Series export: includes series header + all threads.
Thread export: includes only that thread.

sections.title: Multilingual Support
items: Change app language in Settings (Korean / English / Japanese).
All data fields are stored per locale.
Switch locale in the edit dropdown to edit different translations.
If no data exists for current language, fallback order: English → Japanese → Korean.
